title: crataegus monogyna subsp. curvisepala
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. curvisepala, also known as the hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family, native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Crataegus monogyna subsp. curvisepala lie in its flavonoids, phenolic acids, and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, Crataegus monogyna subsp. curvisepala is often used in teas, tinctures, capsules, and extracts, and is traditionally used to support heart health and promote overall well-being.
